allies.rationale_text: Scaffolding helps analogies-and-metaphors work by giving a staged bridge from what the audience already knows to what it needs to learn next.
allies.source_quote: Analogies and metaphors are strongest when people do not yet have a usable mental bucket for the new concept but do have adjacent experience that can carry the explanation.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Analogies-and-metaphors conflicts with abstraction when vivid analogy transfer distorts the structural boundaries of the target domain.
structured_tensions.source_quote: analogies-and-metaphors vs abstraction: analogies-and-metaphors conflicts with abstraction (abstraction) when vivid analogy transfer distorts the structural boundaries of the target domain.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Analogies-and-metaphors conflicts with cognitive-load-theory when layered metaphors increase interpretation burden instead of reducing it.
structured_tensions.source_quote: analogies-and-metaphors vs cognitive-load-theory: analogies-and-metaphors conflicts with cognitive-load-theory (cognitive load theory) when layered metaphors increase interpretation burden instead of reducing it.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the first-order surface tight around one support link and the explicit tension block.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader communication relation sprawl beyond the clearest current-vocabulary seams.
open_questions: Additional allies are source-real, but the current first-order surface is cleanest around scaffolding and the explicit tensions.
